Plaintiffs' Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Aug-19-2021 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than September 2, 2021 plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims as to each defendant. No later than September 2, 2021 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. A case management conference is set for September 9, 2021 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two days prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than October 22, 2021. Responses to the demand are due no later than November 5, 2021. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by November 26, 2021 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference is set for Thursday, December 2, 2021 at 9 a.m. in 603. Personal appearance is required by all unless a party is granted excuse based on good cause. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)

MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Order Advancing The Current Trial Date With Preference; And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Aug-19-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Stephen L. Jenkins of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing via CourtCall for defendant and opposing party Boss Manufacturing Company.
Yen-Yu Liu of BERKES CRANE ROBINSON & SEAL LLP (213-955-1150), appearing via CourtCall for defendant Quad-C Corporation and joinder to defendant Boss Manufacturing Company's opposition.

RULING:
Not contested and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' Motion for Order Advancing the Current Trial Date with Preference; and Extending Discovery Cutoff is GRANTED under C.C.P. section 36(a). Opposition filed by Boss Manufacturing Company with joinder by American Optical Corporation and Quad C corporation. Designated Defense Counsel filed a response to Plaintiff's motion.

The trial date of March 28, 2022 is vacated. The new trial date is December 6, 2021 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. section 36(f) is December 17, 2021. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is December 2, 2021. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is November 19, 2021. Expert discovery cut-off date is December 3, 2021. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than September 17, 2021. If Plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
